summary: Lovelight shines, Bird Song soars, tight club energy throughout
blurb: The set opens with acoustic gems like Cassidy and Ripple before launching into a high‑octane electric half highlighted by Turn On Your Lovelight. Jerry’s fire‑filled guitar work and Phil’s solid bass drive the jams, while Brent’s clean piano adds texture. Lovelight, Bird Song and The Wheel stand out as must‑hear moments.
review: On October 16, 1981 the Dead turned Club Melk Weg into a miniature festival of acoustic charm and electric fireworks. An intimate first set delivered rare treats – On The Road Again, Dire Wolf, Monkey And The Engineer and a beautifully rendered Bird Song that had Phil “tearing it up” in the mix. Cassidy, Oh Babe, It Ain’t No Lie, The Race Is On and Ripple kept the vibe buoyant before the band shifted gears into a blistering second set. A birthday‑charged Bob Weir led the charge with Turn On Your Lovelight, Hully Gully and The Wheel, while Jerry ripped through his borrowed Yamaha gear with a “on fire” solo voice despite a thinner tone and later reduced vocal presence. Phil’s bass stayed rock solid throughout, especially on Bird Song, and Brent’s piano‑only approach gave the club sound a crisp, stripped‑down texture. Highlights like Lovelight, The Wheel and Samson & Delilah make this show a standout club performance, and the Charlie Miller soundboard transfer (gd1981-10-16.sbd.miller.110351.flac16) captures it with crystal‑clear clarity – the definitive listening version for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight of nine recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising Lovelight, Bird Song and the overall high‑energy club vibe. No consistent negative comments appear; the lone mixed review still notes a standout moment. This consensus yields a robust +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard transfer with clean mix, Brent’s keyboards sit nicely and no drop‑outs – captures the full energy of the night.

key_highlights: Turn On Your Lovelight ignites the electric set with birthday‑mode enthusiasm.
Bird Song soars in the acoustic half, showcasing Phil’s bass and Jerry’s fiery lead.
The Wheel and Hully Gully deliver stripped‑down club fireworks.
Acoustic gems Cassidy, Ripple and the rare Monkey And The Engineer add depth.

band_performance.Jerry: Played with fire on borrowed Yamaha gear, delivering scorching solos despite a thinner tone and reduced vocal presence later in the show.
band_performance.Phil: Provided a rock‑solid bass foundation, especially tearing it up on Bird Song; his playing was reliable throughout the night.
band_performance.Bob: In birthday mode, sang energetically (notably on Lovelight) and his guitar cut through clearly in the mix.
band_performance.Brent: Delivered clean piano tones that sat perfectly in the club setting, giving a crisp, stripped‑down texture without overwhelming the band.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
